description.content: Endpoints for downloading full project configuration specs for server-side local evaluation.

Downloads the full project configuration for server-side local evaluation. This endpoint is used by server SDKs to retrieve all gate, config, experiment, and layer definitions for evaluating locally without per-request network calls. This enables sub-millisecond feature gate checks.
Added as a part of security scheme: apikey
Added as a part of security scheme: apikey
Endpoints for fetching dynamic configuration values for users, supporting both dynamic configs and experiments.

item.name: Get a dynamic config or experiment

request.name: Get a dynamic config or experiment

description.content: Fetches the configuration values for a dynamic config or experiment for the specified user. The system automatically determines whether the requested name refers to a dynamic config or an experiment. An exposure event is automatically logged.
Added as a part of security scheme: apikey
Added as a part of security scheme: apikey
Endpoints for logging custom events and exposure data to Statsig for analytics and experiment analysis.

Logs one or more custom events to Statsig for analytics and experiment analysis. Events can include exposure events, custom events with values, and metadata. The STATSIG-CLIENT-TIME header is required to normalize timestamps.

header.description: (Required) Client-side timestamp in milliseconds since epoch, used to normalize event timestamps.

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

header.description: (Required) Client-side timestamp in milliseconds since epoch, used to normalize event timestamps.

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

header.description: (Required) Client-side timestamp in milliseconds since epoch, used to normalize event timestamps.

description.content: Endpoints for retrieving experiment configurations and group assignments for users.

Fetches the experiment configuration and group assignment for the specified user. This is functionally similar to get_config but specifically targets experiments. An exposure event is automatically logged for experiment analysis.
Added as a part of security scheme: apikey
Added as a part of security scheme: apikey
Endpoints for evaluating feature gates for users, returning boolean pass/fail values along with rule identification.
Evaluates a single feature gate for the specified user and returns the boolean pass/fail result along with rule identification. Statsig automatically logs an exposure event for the gate check.
Added as a part of security scheme: apikey
Added as a part of security scheme: apikey
Evaluates multiple feature gates for the specified user in a single request and returns the boolean pass/fail results for each gate. Statsig automatically logs exposure events for each gate check.
Added as a part of security scheme: apikey
Added as a part of security scheme: apikey
Endpoints for initializing client SDKs with all evaluated gates, configs, experiments, and layers for a given user.

Returns all evaluated gates, configs, experiments, and layers for a given user. This is the primary endpoint used by client SDKs for initialization. The response contains hashed names for security, and the client SDK decodes them locally.

response.name: Full set of evaluated configurations for the user

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey
Added as a part of security scheme: apikey
Endpoints for fetching layer parameter values, enabling shared parameters across multiple experiments.
Fetches parameter values from a layer for the specified user. Layers allow sharing parameters across multiple experiments. An exposure event is logged only when a specific parameter is accessed.
Added as a part of security scheme: apikey
Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

The Statsig HTTP API allows developers to evaluate feature gates, dynamic configs, and experiments for users via server-side HTTP requests. It provides endpoints for checking gate values, fetching configuration data, and logging custom events. All requests use the POST method with JSON request bodies and require authentication via the statsig-api-key header. While Statsig recommends using their official SDKs for most use cases, the HTTP API is available for environments where an SDK is not available or when direct HTTP integration is preferred.
